title: Passing a wire wider than the module's argument doesn't trigger an error

features.name: concatenation in module port connection
features.description: Code should include a concatenation expression {a, b} used as an argument in module instantiation where the concatenated result width differs from the target port width
features.snippet:
module m(input [31:0] arg1); endmodule
module top; wire [31:0] xwire; m m1({xwire[31], xwire[31:0]}); endmodule

features.name: concatenation producing wider bit-width than target input
features.description: Code should include a concatenation where the total bit-width exceeds the target input port width by at least one bit
